summary: Conjecture 4 from arXiv:1605.07411 — that Forb(S) is χ-bounded for every oriented star S — remains open in full generality. The source paper proved the cases k=0, k=ℓ=1, and the TT_3-free setting; subsequent work (arXiv:2103.07886) proved the S_2^+ special case (locally out-transitive oriented graphs have dichromatic number at most 2). The related Aboulker–Charbit–Naserasr conjecture for oriented trees has seen additional progress (P4 orientations in arXiv:2209.06171, P6-free triangle-free in arXiv:2212.02272), but the full oriented-star conjecture appears open for general k ≥ 2.

since_posted.title: Decomposing and colouring some locally semicomplete digraphs
since_posted.authors: Pierre Aboulker, Guillaume Aubian, Pierre Charbit
since_posted.claim: Proves the conjecture for the oriented star S_2^+ by showing that every locally out-transitive oriented graph (in which the out-neighbourhood of every vertex induces a transitive tournament) has dichromatic number at most 2.

internal_refs_verified.note: WebFetch shows the paper proves that oriented graphs with no induced directed path on six vertices and no triangle have bounded dichromatic number — a result about P6-free digraphs, not directly about oriented stars. The internal-ref description attributing progress on Conjecture 4 appears to conflate this with the broader Aboulker–Charbit–Naserasr conjecture on oriented trees.

WebFetch confirmed: paper proves that locally out-transitive oriented graphs (out-neighbourhood always a transitive tournament) can be 2-coloured acyclically, consistent with the S_2^+ special case of Conjecture 4.

search_queries: oriented star Forb chi-bounded oriented graphs Aboulker 1605.07411
Forb oriented star chi-bounded dichromatic number S_k,k Gyarfas-Sumner oriented graphs
notes: No paper resolving the full conjecture was found. Special cases proven in the source paper (k=0, k=ℓ=1, TT_3-free) and in arXiv:2103.07886 (S_2^+ case). The closely related conjecture on oriented trees (Aboulker–Charbit–Naserasr) has seen active progress through 2023, but Conjecture 4 for general oriented stars S_{k,k} with k ≥ 2 appears open.
